name: Rep. Dan Kildee
tweet: PFAS exposure has been linked to many health issues, including cancer, high cholesterol, weakened immune system & hypertension during pregnancy. But for many veterans exposed to #PFAS, the military has refused to cover these health issues as service-related issues.

name: Rep. Dan Kildee
tweet: The VETS PFAS Act would require the VA to cover treatment of certain health conditions related to #PFAS exposure. Under our bill, PFAS exposure, as a part of military service, would be considered a service-connected disability, making veterans eligible for disability & VA care.

name: Rep. Dan Kildee
tweet: @Bethany @DHSgov @HHSGov This entire humanitarian crisis is heartbreaking. Kids should be with their families, not separated in some far away facility. Thousands of children have suffered and will forever be scarred by the Trump Administration’s inhumane separation of families.

name: Rep. Dan Kildee
tweet: @Bethany @DHSgov @HHSGov I have also introduced legislation, the Keep Families Together Act, that would permanently stop the Trump Administration from separating families. Congress should act on this bill without delay. #FamiliesBelongTogther

name: Rep. Dan Kildee
tweet: @Bethany @DHSgov @HHSGov A federal judge ordered the Trump Administration to reunite children with their families by July 10th (if under 5-years-old) or July 26th (if over the age of 5). @DHSgov & @HHSGov must prioritize the reunification of families to meet these deadlines.

name: Rep. Dan Kildee
tweet: @Bethany @DHSgov @HHSGov On our tour, @Bethany told me that these children who have been separated from their families are experiencing high levels of trauma. The youngest kid I saw today was just 6 mos. old.

name: Rep. Dan Kildee
tweet: @Bethany @DHSgov @HHSGov So far a handful, but clearly not a majority, of the children at @Bethany have been reunited with their families. They are currently working to reunite families with little direction or resources from the Trump Administration. There is little urgency from @HHSgov and @DHSgov.

name: Rep. Dan Kildee
tweet: @Bethany With that said, I remain concerned that the Trump Administration—incl. @DHSgov & @HHSGov—does not have a coherent plan to quickly reunite children with their families. Facilities like @Bethany are receiving little or no direction from the federal govt. regarding reunification.

name: Rep. Dan Kildee
tweet: @Bethany I want to thank @Bethany for providing excellent care for children currently in their facilities. While the U.S. shouldn't have had a policy of separating children & families in the first place, the dozens of children we saw are receiving quality care (cont.)

name: Rep. Dan Kildee
tweet: Understandably, for the privacy of the children, no photos/videos were allowed on our tour today. I appreciate @Bethany allowing a bipartisan delegation of Members to visit their facilities to see and interact with children who have been separated from their families. (cont).

name: Rep. Dan Kildee
tweet: Just left Bethany Christian Services in Grand Rapids, where I was able to meet with children who have been separated from their families because of the Trump's Administration's immigration policies.   We need to make sure families are reunited because #FamiliesBelongTogether.
